places.name: ABRASAMAR
places.vibe: Gulf-of-Mexico-style seafood with viral towers under a retractable gravel-floor roof.

places.name: Ostrería 109
places.vibe: Oyster bar and grill on Nuevo León focused on fresh, seasonal Mexican seafood.

places.name: Contramar
places.vibe: The Michelin Bib Gourmand palapa institution since 1998 — go for the pink-sauce tuna tostadas.

places.name: Mi Compa Chava Marisquería
places.vibe: High-energy Sinaloa-style marisquería with banda music, caguamas, and towering seafood platters.

places.name: Smart Food Pescado Sustentable
places.vibe: CDMX's first fully sustainable fish market, with full traceability on every catch.

places.name: EL TIBUR
places.vibe: Barrio seafood spot mixing Oaxaca, Peru, and Asia influences into a casual raw bar.

places.name: Migo Bagels
places.vibe: New-school bagel shop on Jalapa turning out proper everything bagels.

places.name: Mendl Delicatessen
places.vibe: Jewish deli famous for bagels and smoked salmon, a brunch spot that's hosted the odd celebrity.

places.name: Panadería Rosetta
places.vibe: The guava pastry that broke the internet — Elena Reygadas' plant-filled bakery icon.

places.name: Churrería El Moro
places.vibe: 24-hour churros-and-hot-chocolate institution on Eje Central, going strong since 1935.

places.name: Cardamomo
places.vibe: Cozy French-Mexican brunch spot on Culiacán known for chilaquiles and artisanal pastries.

places.name: Amorino
places.vibe: Italian gelato hand-shaped into rose 'flowers' — the photogenic scoop of CDMX.

places.name: La Belle Epoque
places.vibe: French fine-dining newcomer with red walls and a chandelier; chef Max Righi's tarte tatin is the move.

places.name: Happy Fruit
places.vibe: Cheerful fruit-themed spot on Campeche for fresh salads, smoothies, and vegan bowls.

places.name: The Green Corner
places.vibe: Mostly-vegan organic market-restaurant serving healthy juices and dishes like enmoladas.

places.name: Blend Station
places.vibe: Pet-friendly specialty coffee spot on Tamaulipas known for smoothies and outdoor seating.

places.name: CounterCulture Club Roma Norte
places.vibe: Coffee, smoothies, and high vibes at this San Luis Potosí corner spot.

places.name: Tomasa Condesa
places.vibe: Pink-toned bakery famous for conchas stuffed with chilaquiles or cochinita pibil.

places.name: Café Drama
places.vibe: A modest café facade hides a wild karaoke-and-cocktails night club behind a secret door.

places.name: Cafe Ocaso
places.vibe: Low-key neighborhood coffee spot on Álvaro Obregón for a chill afternoon.

places.name: Café de Nadie
places.vibe: Day café that flips into a vinyl-and-cocktails bar by night, walk-ins only, no reservations.

places.name: Antonella Caffè
places.vibe: Cozy yet sophisticated all-day café on Álvaro Obregón beloved for long brunches.

places.name: MOMO Coffee - Roma Norte
places.vibe: Open-air, pet-friendly corner café on Jalapa known for horchata coffee and easy mornings.

places.name: Niddo Café Polanco
places.vibe: Sleek specialty-coffee mini-chain that's become a go-to for CDMX remote workers.

places.name: Alma Mia Condesa
places.vibe: Bright brunch café where the chilaquiles and French toast keep the regulars coming back.

places.name: oly.
places.vibe: Casual-elegant Mediterranean neighborhood spot on Alfonso Reyes, known for lemon pasta.

places.name: Cafebrería El Péndulo Condesa
places.vibe: Legendary bookstore-café stacked with novels and plants, a Condesa institution since 1993.

places.name: Cafe Trucha
places.vibe: No-reservations café on Plaza Río de Janeiro known for daily brunch and cake orders.

places.name: Casa Café
places.vibe: Family-run, homey café pouring Chiapas coffee with standout avocado toast.

places.name: Qūentin Café
places.vibe: The CDMX coffee mini-chain that started here and now spans Roma, Condesa, and Juárez.

places.name: Cafeteria Ideal
places.vibe: Design-forward café-bar inside the Pensión guesthouse, playing off 85 years of history.

places.name: Derba Matcha Café
places.vibe: Matcha-focused café inside the Andaz Hotel, big on natural light and photogenic drinks.

places.name: Owa Wellness
places.vibe: New members-club-style wellness reset that opened in Roma for CDMX's health-conscious crowd.

places.name: HiHi Nails Roma
places.vibe: Trendy nail studio on Tabasco known for clean, minimalist manicure sets.

places.name: Rush Nail Bar
places.vibe: Boutique nail bar with a minimalist look and a loyal Roma Norte and Polanco following.

places.name: Caché Self-Care Bar
places.vibe: Self-care-first nail and beauty bar where every treatment comes with a coffee or matcha.

places.name: ROSA BB NAILBAR
places.vibe: Chic little nail bar on Puebla street with a devoted local following.

places.name: K salon
places.vibe: Understated neighborhood hair-and-beauty salon tucked into the Hipódromo.

places.name: Dos Flamingos Studio & Spa
places.vibe: Vegan hair, nail, and spa studio on Coahuila run by master stylists using Aveda and OPI.

places.name: Youth for us Condesa [Recovery] + Hydrafacial
places.vibe: Biohacking wellness studio with sauna, cold plunge, and a Hydrafacial recovery menu.

places.name: Manicura Le Beauty Studio
places.vibe: Russian manicure specialists run by two Ukrainian beauty entrepreneurs on Álvaro Obregón.

places.name: Koti Wellness Roma Norte - Contrast Therapy | Sauna & Cold Plunge | Masajes
places.vibe: Intimate contrast-therapy spot on Coahuila with private sauna and cold-plunge rooms.

places.name: Hiya Wine Bar
places.vibe: A sake-and-natural-wine bar tucked inside Yoru Sushi, made for raw plates and late-night robata.

places.name: Fournier Rousseau
places.vibe: A French pastry café on Álvaro Obregón that doubles as one of the city's go-to natural wine stops.

places.name: PLONK
places.vibe: Chef Flor Camorlinga's Bib Gourmand open-kitchen spot pours mostly Old World, minimal-intervention wine.

places.name: Pisca
places.vibe: Sunny Condesa brunch spot with avocado toast by day and a well-picked wine list by night.

places.name: Hugo
places.vibe: Darkly romantic natural wine den on Veracruz street, French-New York small plates, ~100 labels deep.

places.name: Vigneron
places.vibe: A Bib Gourmand natural wine reference point on Jalapa street, big on small French producers.

places.name: Despacho Margarita
places.vibe: The Licorería Limantour team's ode to the margarita, agave archive and frozen machine included.

places.name: Essex
places.vibe: Electronic-music nightclub in a restored century-old brothel, craft cocktails and big-name DJs.

places.name: Bar Liebre
places.vibe: Under-the-radar Roma Norte pour house—quality drinks, no hype, mostly locals.

places.name: DACOPA
places.vibe: New-wave terrace bar on Álvaro Obregón for draft cocktails and tight bites.

places.name: Carajo Maria
places.vibe: Contemporary cantina reinvention in Juárez—unpretentious drinks, good music, no fuss.

places.name: Owl Bar (Speakeasy)
places.vibe: Minimalist Condesa speakeasy with genuinely creative author cocktails and house music.

places.name: ololo
places.vibe: Orange-hued speakeasy above NIICH spinning vinyl and digital sets with mezcal-forward drinks.

places.name: Roca
places.vibe: Audiophile listening bar with a custom sound system, turntables, and a killer martini.

places.name: Bar Mauro
places.vibe: 1970s-Italy-inspired bar ranked #2 in North America's 50 Best—reserve by DM.

places.name: Salón Palomilla
places.vibe: Live jazz under a dramatic ceiling oculus, with espresso martinis and mezcal-heavy pours.

places.name: Kinshasa Roma
places.vibe: Retractable-roof rooftop above Mercado Roma, all Afro/melodic house till 2am.

places.name: Tlecan
places.vibe: Tiny mezcalería ranked among North America's best bars—13 small-batch mezcals, standing room only.

places.name: Bar Anchoa
places.vibe: Tiny neighborhood bar for negronis and palomas, dog-friendly, zero pretension.

places.name: Caiman
places.vibe: One of the last real bars in Condesa—natural wine, sharp classics, a nostalgic 90s playlist.

places.name: Balagán
places.vibe: Plant-filled Juárez patio for Mediterranean bites, DJs, and a retractable glass roof.

places.name: PETRA
places.vibe: Gastro-bar 'liquid laboratory' with hi-fi sound and double-distilled house spirits.

places.name: Rosso
places.vibe: Red-lit hidden speakeasy inside Pavorosso's kitchen—ask the host for access.

places.name: Barra Lupe
places.vibe: Cantina reinvented around nixtamalized corn, with author cocktails honoring sotol and menyul.

places.name: Yagé
places.vibe: Small Condesa space mixing cooking, art, mezcal, and wine into one late-night hang.

places.name: Zimo
places.vibe: Fresh-pasta Italian wine bar with vinyl on the turntable and cocktails on the side.

places.name: Disco Café & Bar
places.vibe: Retro disco-ball café by day, low-key bar by night, big on nostalgia and warm lighting.

places.name: Pandora Roof Top
places.vibe: Art-deco rooftop in Nápoles/Juárez for cocktails, good food, and skyline views.

places.name: Maleza
places.vibe: Farm-driven Condesa kitchen and bar spotlighting small Valle de México producers.

places.name: Terraza Coraje
places.vibe: Cozy rooftop terrace with nightly DJs, Mexican-Asian bites, and a solid wine list.

places.name: Anónimo
places.vibe: Condesa corner spot that turns from pasta lunches into natural-wine and cocktail nights.

places.name: Altanera Roma
places.vibe: Penthouse rooftop over Roma with sweeping CDMX views and contemporary Mexican plates.

places.name: Toledo Rooftop
places.vibe: Jungle-decked rooftop over Reforma's skyline, upbeat house music, big-name guest DJs.

places.name: Terraza Gran Hotel
places.vibe: Fifth-floor terrace inside the historic Gran Hotel with views over the Zócalo and Catedral.

places.name: El Tigre Silencioso
places.vibe: Gourmet cantina in a 1914 mansion—vermouth on tap and serious bar snacks.

places.name: Xaman Bar
places.vibe: Unmarked basement bar with a shamanic theme, smoke-heavy drinks, and underground house.

places.name: Colonia Meadery
places.vibe: Mexico's first meadery—house-brewed hidromiel alongside Mexican-barbecue bites.

places.name: Ricochet Apero
places.vibe: New apero bar channeling 1970s aperitivo culture on Chapultepec—clean, confident, low-key.

places.name: La Romana
places.vibe: Italodisco on the speakers, high-end cocktails with a Mexican twist, nightly till 2am.

places.name: TRAMPA
places.vibe: Drinks-and-tapas corner spot on Alfonso Reyes that always takes walk-ins.

places.name: RUIDO
places.vibe: The dinner-to-club pipeline done right — mezcal, light bites, then the music takes over.

places.name: Tokyo Music Bar
places.vibe: Hi-fi vinyl, Japanese whisky and gold barstools in Little Tokyo, from the Edo Kobayashi group.

places.name: Jazzatlán Capital
places.vibe: Live jazz every single night, the CDMX outpost of Puebla's long-running Jazzatlán club.

places.name: Departamento
places.vibe: Rooftop-and-indoor complex on Álvaro Obregón mixing DJ sets and live music for a no-pretense crowd.

places.name: VOID
places.vibe: Eleven-year-old DnB and techno stronghold tucked into a Doctores warehouse.

places.name: Fónico
places.vibe: Northwestern Mexican cooking from chef Billy Maldonado with live music setting the pace.

places.name: sona - listening space
places.vibe: No phones, no talking, no alcohol — just deep listening on a serious hi-fi rig in Hipódromo Condesa.

places.name: L'enfant
places.vibe: Chef Aram Abisahi's French brasserie on Casa Prim's 3rd floor, with a secret rooftop terrace.

places.name: CASTIZO ROMA
places.vibe: Chef Milu Crespo's contemporary Spanish-Mediterranean cooking in a Condesa mansion.

places.name: El Jamil
places.vibe: Family-run Lebanese kitchen since 2006 — go for the hummus and grape leaves.

places.name: Fugu Sushi
places.vibe: Walk-in-only Edomae sushi bar cutting fish at an intimate counter — no reservations.

places.name: Darosa
places.vibe: Tables piled with Italian dishes made for sharing in a Juárez townhouse.

places.name: Étranger
places.vibe: Chef Rodrigo Gallardo's small, precise French bistronomy that shifts with the day's ingredients.

places.name: ARDA
places.vibe: Smoke-and-fire cooking from chefs Germán Caraballo and Luis Solano inside a historic Roma mansion.

places.name: Ticuchi
places.vibe: Enrique Olvera's cave-like agave bar on Pujol's original site — mezcal and Oaxacan snacks in near-darkness.

places.name: Rosa Negra
places.vibe: Steakhouse-meets-nightclub on Masaryk — Latin American grill, live shows, big scene energy.

places.name: Animal Masaryk
places.vibe: Charcoal-grilled meats meet Mexican-Japanese flavors, with live music turning dinner into a party.

places.name: Propio
places.vibe: Young husband-and-wife chef duo's open-kitchen newcomer — creative plates, sit at the counter if you can.

places.name: Orbita
places.vibe: Retro coffee bar by day, DJ-fueled cocktail bar by night — walk-ins only.

places.name: Em
places.vibe: Lucho Martínez's Michelin-starred ode to Veracruz — a nine-course tasting menu that tells a story.

places.name: Havre 77
places.vibe: Eduardo García's ode to French cuisine in a 19th-century house — onion soup, oysters, duck.

places.name: Migrante
places.vibe: Chef Fernando Martínez's Michelin-listed mashup of Mexican, Asian and French technique.

places.name: Huset
places.vibe: "Cocina de campo" built around a wood-fired grill in a leafy Porfirian mansion patio.

places.name: Quintonil
places.vibe: Jorge Vallejo's tasting-menu temple, a Pujol alum turned World's 50 Best regular — book weeks out.

places.name: Lardo
places.vibe: Elena Reygadas' casual Rosetta sibling — croque monsieur, pizzas and Sunday brunch queues.

places.name: Balcón del Zócalo
places.vibe: Chef Pepe Salinas' Mexican fine dining on a rooftop balcony overlooking the Cathedral and Zócalo.

places.name: Blanco Colima
places.vibe: Unpretentious upscale bistro in a Porfirian mansion, with one of the city's deepest mezcal lists.

places.name: Restaurante Rosetta
places.vibe: Elena Reygadas' Michelin-starred Mexican-Mediterranean landmark — the guava bread alone is worth it.

places.name: San Ángel Inn
places.vibe: Colonial hacienda dating to 1963, favorite of politicians — classic Mexican dishes amid gardens.

places.name: Botánico
places.vibe: Modern bistro fare served around a secret cactus garden and koi pond.

places.name: FERAL
places.vibe: Chef Paola Sepúlveda's French-Oriental-Mexican mashup in a lively, chic dining room.

places.name: Baldío
places.vibe: Mexico's first zero-waste restaurant, CDMX's only Michelin Green Star — regenerative, farmer-first cooking.

places.name: Wagyu Jyube
places.vibe: Mexico's first 100% Japanese wagyu omakase counter.

places.name: Maizajo
places.vibe: Cult tortillería reviving heirloom criollo corn — tacos, tlacoyos and totopos made from their own nixtamal.

places.name: Sartoria
places.vibe: Contemporary Italian osteria where fresh pasta is treated like ritual — often called CDMX's best Italian.

places.name: Boogie's Pizza
places.vibe: Walk-in-only NY-style slices from an American pizza expat until the dough runs out.

places.name: El Olvidado
places.vibe: An English-inspired bistro-café serving slow-made European plates in a quiet Coyoacán corner.

places.name: Gaba Restaurante
places.vibe: A daytime café that flips into bold, inventive cocina de autor once the sun goes down.

places.name: Travieso Travieso
places.vibe: Weekly-changing menus, guest-chef pop-ups and live DJs keep this Roma spot unpredictable.

places.name: HOMARE Cocina Tradicional Japonesa
places.vibe: A 12-seat counter where a veteran Osaka-trained chef runs unpretentious, fish-flown-from-Japan omakase.

places.name: Aúna Restaurante
places.vibe: Chef Fernando Torres's Michelin-starred take on elevated, contemporary Mexican comfort food.

places.name: Casa Elena Restaurante
places.vibe: A pink-tinged Condesa bakery-turned-restaurant serving homey Mexican cooking with a design-forward glow.

places.name: Restaurante Castizo
places.vibe: Chef Milu Crespo's Spanish-Mediterranean menu anchors this all-day Condesa favorite.

places.name: Taquería "Sin Nombre"
places.vibe: A nameless, low-key Roma taquería reinterpreting classic tacos late into the night.

places.name: Taverna
places.vibe: Mediterranean smoke cookery from Grupo Archipiélago inside a beautifully restored 1900s Juárez house.

places.name: Estiatorio Nostos (Lomas)
places.vibe: Real deal Greek seafood and good wine for long, lingering lunches in Lomas.

places.name: Casa Visconti
places.vibe: Italian gelato done properly, scooped at a string of cult little Condesa and Coyoacán windows.

places.name: Bartola
places.vibe: Italo-American bistro comfort food with charm, chicken parm included, on Roma Norte's Tabasco street.

places.name: Cursi
places.vibe: A romantic little steakhouse on Chihuahua street that's become one of Roma's hardest-to-book new spots.

places.name: Lotti
places.vibe: Swiss chef Luc Liebster reworks European tradition with Mexican ingredients inside a 1915 Porfirian mansion.

places.name: La Pantera Asador
places.vibe: Fire, firewood and a hidden patio from the Campobaja crew — order the tacos and linger.

places.name: Babero
places.vibe: Trendy Italian plates like ibérico croquetas and seafood risotto in a buzzy Roma Norte room.

places.name: Tosco Restaurante
places.vibe: One of the Juárez neighborhood's newest openings, already drawing a loyal after-work crowd.

places.name: Biggie’s
places.vibe: A Notorious B.I.G.-inspired bistro pouring house IPA and negronis under a hip-hop bodega vibe.

places.name: Amïn
places.vibe: A gourmet neighborhood café on Michoacán street good for breakfast through dinner.

places.name: Taquería Orinoco
places.vibe: The Monterrey-style taco chain that built a cult following one queue-out-the-door location at a time.

places.name: Librería Valladolid
places.vibe: Roma's newest speakeasy — a bookshop by day, hidden cocktail bar with canned bites by night.

places.name: Armario Comunal
places.vibe: Condesa's go-to secondhand shop — vintage and thrifted pieces from $50 pesos up.

places.name: Farmacia Internacional
places.vibe: An old pharmacy reborn as an all-day café — expect a line for brunch on weekends.

places.name: Perfumérica Condesa
places.vibe: Feels like a perfume lab — sniff testers through brass horns to find your signature scent.

places.name: Fueguia 1833 Mexico
places.vibe: Argentine niche perfumery on Córdoba — botanical fragrances built like tiny art pieces.

places.name: Aurelia Concept Store
places.vibe: Sister-run concept store stocking handmade goods from artisans across Mexico.

places.name: 180º Shop
places.vibe: Concept store and workshop reworking Mexican craft into original, wearable design.

places.name: Botánica Granel
places.vibe: Zero-waste bulk market — bring your own jars for grains, teas, and bar shampoo.

places.name: Viejo Amor Boutique Condesa
places.vibe: Bohemian womenswear mixing vintage finds with contemporary pieces on Atlixco.

places.name: RETOP : RENTAL, VINTAGE AND LOCAL STORE
places.vibe: Rent designer fashion instead of buying it — vintage and local labels too.

places.name: Biblioteca Vasconcelos
places.vibe: Floating steel bookshelves and a suspended whale skeleton — modern Mexico's cathedral of books.

places.name: Tepotzotlán
places.vibe: Baroque ex-convent turned museum, plus one of Mexico's most gilded churrigueresque façades.

places.name: Palacio de Bellas Artes
places.vibe: Art deco/nouveau marble palace — Tiffany glass curtain, murals by Rivera and Orozco inside.

places.name: LagoAlgo
places.vibe: Lakeside dining and rotating chef residencies inside Chapultepec Park's midcentury pavilion.

places.name: Frida Kahlo Museum
places.vibe: La Casa Azul — Kahlo's cobalt-blue home, studio, and garden where it all happened.

places.name: Museo Soumaya
places.vibe: Free entry into a hexagon-clad silver blob housing Rodins and 30 centuries of art.

places.name: National Art Museum
places.vibe: Mexican art from viceregal times to the 20th century inside a Beaux-Arts former palace.

places.name: Chapultepec Castle
places.vibe: The only royal castle in the Americas — Maximilian's residence, now a history museum with skyline views.

places.name: Parque Quetzalcoatl
places.vibe: Newly reopened park by the Toltec ruins — feathered-serpent fountains and family-friendly space.

places.name: La Gruta
places.vibe: Buffet lunch inside an actual cave steps from the Teotihuacán pyramids — touristy but memorable.

places.name: Nido de Quetzalcoatl
places.vibe: Javier Senosiain's snaking, mosaic-covered organic architecture — like stepping into a dream.

places.name: Xochimilco
places.vibe: Colorful trajineras drift through Aztec-era canals — mariachi, micheladas, and chaos included.

places.name: The House of Tiles
places.vibe: Talavera-tiled 18th-century palace turned Sanborns — an Orozco mural over the breakfast crowd.

places.name: Alae's Art Room
places.vibe: Pole and aerial fitness studio in Roma Norte — strength training that feels like performance art.

places.name: The Wild Oscar
places.vibe: Quiet aparthotel near Auditorio Nacional — built for travelers who want to work in peace.

places.name: Casa Simera
places.vibe: Viral café with pilates upstairs — matcha, lavender-lemon pancakes, and a line out the door.

places.name: Haab Project Condesa
places.vibe: Members' coworking club on Amsterdam — restaurant, events, and design-forward workspaces.
